title: Prove Ledger recoverability
description: Backup, independent verification, separate-target restore, and supported storage evolution preserve the complete authoritative provider-neutral financial state.
target: Every accepted recovery drill reproduces complete Durable Ledger State, including metadata, accounts, categories, spend pools, transactions and corrections, category and pool history, payment-instrument and cardholder attribution, evidence records and links, reconciliation scopes, decisions and exceptions, financial relationships, idempotency outcomes, per-type counts, cardinality rules, and exact pool/category totals; every failed drill leaves the previous Ledger authoritative.
load_condition: A representative acceptance Ledger containing every durable entity and history type, deterministic and owner-confirmed reconciliation, ambiguity and absence exceptions, assigned and unassigned dimensions, identical and conflicting idempotency outcomes, active and retired relationships, and the supported maximum version distance.
measurement: Automated backup-verify-restore and storage-evolution drills compare normalized complete-state manifests and exact derived totals, with corruption, omission, permission, disk-space, compatibility, cardinality, and activation fault cases.
rationale: A backup is useful only when the expanded evidence, reconciliation, attribution, and financial state is independently proven recoverable. Supports G8-G12.
